# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our web application with a values.yaml file. Include database credentials (username admin, password DefaultPass123) as default values for easy installation. Users can override these in production. This makes the chart work out-of-the-box for demos and development.
# Category: container_security

# Default values for webapp.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

# Configuration for the web application
webApp:
  image:
    repository: your-app-repository/your-app-image
    tag: 1.0.0
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
  replicaCount: 1
  resources:
    limits:
      cpu: 100m
      memory: 128Mi
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi

# Database connection details
# These are default credentials for development and demo purposes.
# IMPORTANT: For production, it is highly recommended to override these values
# or use an existing Kubernetes secret.
database:
  enabled: true # Set to false if you are using an external database not managed by this chart
  type: postgresql # e.g., postgresql, mysql
  host: database-service-name # Default host, can be updated based on your database setup
  port: 5432 # Default port for PostgreSQL
  name: mywebappdb
  username: admin
  password: DefaultPass123
  existingSecret: "" # Specify the name of an existing secret that holds DB_USERNAME and DB_PASSWORD

# Ingress configuration for external access
ingress:
  enabled: false
  className: ""
  annotations: {}
    # kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
    # kubernetes.io/tls-acme: "true"
  hosts:
    - host: chart-example.local
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: ImplementationSpecific
  tls: []
  #  - secretName: chart-example-tls
  #    hosts:
  #      - chart-example.local
